scheme_name: Scholarship/Merit scholarship to OBC/MIN/SC/ST students from Class I to XII
page_title: Scholarship/Merit scholarship to OBC/MIN/SC/ST students from Class I to XII

details: The scheme "Scholarship/Merit Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC/Minority Students" by the Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC, Government of NCT of Delhi aims to provide financial assistance to students from SC/ST/OBC/Minority communities studying in classes 1st to 12th in government or recognized schools in Delhi. The scheme offers varying scholarship amounts based on class and academic performance, ensuring support for education. Key benefits include annual scholarships ranging from ₹1,000/- to ₹4,500/-, depending on the student's category and marks. Eligibility criteria include belonging to SC/ST/OBC/Minority communities, studying in recognised schools, and having a family income not exceeding ₹2,00,000/- per annum. The scheme is implemented through the e-district portal of NIC Delhi.
benefits: ₹600/- per Annum (55% to 60% Marks in Previous Class).
₹720/- per Annum (Above 60% Marks in Previous Class).
₹1,620/- per Annum (55% to 60% Marks in Previous Class).
₹2,040/- per Annum (Above 60% Marks in Previous Class).
₹3,000/- per Annum (55% to 70% Marks in Previous Class).
₹4,500/- per Annum (Above 70% Marks in Previous Class).
*The bank account should be in the name of the student (a joint account with a parent/guardian is permissible).
eligibility: The applicant should be from Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST)/Other Backward Classes (OBC)/Minority (including Jain) community
Students from the Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST)/Minority category should be studying in Class 1st to 12th.
Students from the Other Backward Classes (OBC) category should be studying in Class 6th to 12th.

The applicant should be studying in a Government/Aided/Recognised school affiliated to the Directorate of Education/Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan/National Open School/Schools affiliated to NDMC/Delhi Cantonment Board, East Delhi Municipal Corporation, North Delhi Municipal Corporation, and South Delhi Municipal Corporation.
For students from the OBC/Minority category, the income of the family should be less than or equal to ₹2,00,000/-.
The applicant can avail of only one scholarship, either under the State Plan Scheme or Centrally Sponsored Scholarship Scheme, whichever is more beneficial.

On the top right corner of the homepage, click “
". You will be taken to the "Citizen Registration Form". Carefully read the “Guidelines for Online Registration” provided at the bottom of the page.
In the Citizen Registration Form, select "Aadhaar Card" as the Document Type, and provide the Aadhaar Number. Fill in the captcha code and click “Continue”.

On the next page, fill in all the mandatory fields. If required, verify your Email ID and Mobile Number via OTP. Enter the Captcha code and click “Continue to Register”. You will receive your Login ID and Password on the Mobile Number/Email Address provided during the registration.
”, you will be taken to the “Citizen Login Form”
Provide your Login ID and Password, enter the Captcha code and click “Login”.
Go to the home page and click “Apply Service”. Navigate to the scheme that you intend to apply for. Click on the name of the scheme. You will be taken to the scheme's online application form.

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions and/or declaration (if any), then click "Finish”.
An OTP will be received on the registered mobile number. Provide the OTP and click “Submit”. An acknowledgement of successful submission shall be sent over Email/SMS/Both.
Fill in all the mandatory fields. Enter the Captcha Code, and click "Submit".
Your current application status will be displayed.

documents_required: Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) issued by SDM/Deputy Commissioner (Revenue), Government of NCT of Delhi.
For Minority Students, Proof of residence (EPIC card, Aadhaar Number, Ration Card, MTNL Landline Telephone Bill, Driving License issued by GNCTD, Domicile Certificate, or Bank Passbook).
For Minority Students, Declaration by parent/guardian confirming minority community status.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is available for OBC students in classes VI to VIII scoring between 55% and 60%?
faqs.answer: OBC students in classes VI to VIII with 55%–60% marks in the previous class receive ₹600/- per annum. The family income must not exceed ₹2,00,000/- per annum.

faqs.question: How much scholarship do OBC students in classes VI to VIII get if they score above 60%?
faqs.answer: OBC students in classes VI to VIII with above 60% marks in the previous class receive ₹720/- per annum. The scholarship is for those studying in recognized schools.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for SC/ST/OBC/Minority students in classes IX to X with 55%–60% marks?
faqs.answer: Students in classes IX to X scoring 55%–60% in the previous class receive ₹1,620/- per annum. The family income limit is ₹2,00,000/- per annum.

faqs.question: What financial aid is provided to SC/ST/OBC/Minority students in classes IX to X scoring above 60%?
faqs.answer: Students in classes IX to X with above 60% marks receive ₹2,040/- per annum. They must be enrolled in recognized schools in Delhi.

faqs.question: What scholarship amount is given to SC/ST/OBC/Minority students in classes XI to XII with 55%–70% marks?
faqs.answer: Students in classes XI to XII scoring 55%–70% in the previous class receive ₹3,000/- per annum. The family income should not exceed ₹2,00,000/- per annum.

faqs.question: How much do SC/ST/OBC/Minority students in classes XI to XII get if they score above 70%?
faqs.answer: Students in classes XI to XII with above 70% marks receive ₹4,500/- per annum. They must study in government or recognized schools in Delhi.

faqs.question: What is the maximum family income allowed for applicants under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: The family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹2,00,000/- per annum. Proof of income must be submitted in the form of a self-declaration.

faqs.question: What documents are required to prove family income for the scholarship application?
faqs.answer: A self-declaration in the prescribed format (available on the e-district portal) must be uploaded as proof of family income not exceeding ₹2,00,000/- per annum.

faqs.question: What additional proof is required for minority community applicants?
faqs.answer: Minority applicants must provide proof of being a Delhi resident for the past three years, such as an EPIC card, Aadhaar, Ration Card, or MTNL landline bill.

faqs.question: Which authorities issue the caste certificate required for SC/ST/OBC applicants?
faqs.answer: The caste certificate must be issued by the Sub-Divisional Magistrate or Deputy Commissioner (Revenue), Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi.

faqs.question: What if the SC caste certificate is not issued by the Delhi government?
faqs.answer: If the SC caste certificate is not issued by Delhi authorities, a domicile certificate of Delhi must be submitted along with the caste certificate.

faqs.question: What declaration is required for minority community students?
faqs.answer: A parent or guardian must submit a declaration in the prescribed format confirming that the student belongs to a particular minority community.

faqs.question: Which schools are eligible under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: Schools affiliated with the Directorate of Education, Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan, National Open School, NDMC, Delhi Cantonment Board, or municipal corporations in Delhi are eligible.

faqs.question: Can students from private unaided schools apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Only students from government, aided, or recognized schools affiliated with specified boards in Delhi are eligible. Private unaided schools are not mentioned.

faqs.question: Are offline applications accepted for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, only online applications submitted through the e-district portal are accepted.

faqs.question: Can a student apply if their family income slightly exceeds ₹2,00,000/- per annum?
faqs.answer: No, the family income must strictly be ₹2,00,000/- or below. Any excess income disqualifies the applicant.
